hero.title: All your VPS and CLIs
hero.lede: One CLI subscription. Every server you own. Voice control from anywhere — your phone is the IDE now.
hero.metaLine: Open source for self-host · Mobile-first · Voice input

mockup.userMsg: Ship the dark-mode PR to staging
mockup.asstFinal: Deployed. Smoke tests green. Closing the app — agent will keep going.
mockup.composer: Reply or hold mic to speak…

transit.title: Idea hits in transit.
transit.body: You jot down "do it tonight" — by night you don't even remember why.

prod2am.title: Prod breaks at 2am.
prod2am.body: Time to dig out the laptop, SSH in, debug by hand — long after you should be sleeping.

sub200.title: One $200 sub, three servers.
sub200.body: You bought a CLI subscription. You have three servers. It only runs on one of them.

botsFail.title: Telegram bots fall over.
botsFail.body: They crash, lose context, die after 5 minutes mid-task — and pretend they finished with a fake "✅ done".

howItWorks.title: Three steps and you're running.
howItWorks.lede: One curl on each box, one web app on every screen.

step1.title: Install on any server
step1.body: One curl. Self-host on your own VPS — free forever, no telemetry, no limits.

step2.title: Plug in your CLIs
step2.body: Install Claude Code, Gemini CLI or any other agent on your servers. Authenticate once — then drive everything from the app.

step3.title: Open it on your phone
step3.body: All your VPS and projects in one place. Kick off tasks, monitor progress, dictate prompts by voice.
step3.chatUser: Roll back the last deploy on prod-fra-1
step3.chatAsstSuffix: and re-running migrations. I'll keep going if you close the app.

features.title: Built for the way you actually work now.

oneCli.title: One CLI, every server
oneCli.body: Plug a CLI into one box — it works across the rest through a built-in proxy. One subscription instead of three.

async.title: Async — fire and forget
async.body: Kick off a task, close the app, walk away. The agent keeps running on your server for as long as needed.

voice.title: Voice input
voice.body: Typing long prompts on a phone is painful. Hold the mic, dictate, send. Audio stays in your browser.

multiVendor.title: Multi-vendor
multiVendor.body: Claude Code and Gemini CLI today. Codex, Aider, Cursor CLI on the roadmap. Same UI, different brains.

yourKeys.title: Your keys, your servers
yourKeys.body: OAuth tokens, API keys, agent context — all stay in your infrastructure. No third-party broker, ever.

multiProject.title: Multi-project
multiProject.body: Any number of projects on any number of servers, all in one mobile dashboard. Switch context with a tap.

pricing.title: Run it yourself, or let us run it for you.
pricing.lede: Honest note: this is built by one person. Self-host is free forever with no feature-gating. The hosted tier is how the project gets maintained.

selfHost.name: Self-host
selfHost.blurb: Bring your own VPS. Clone, configure, ship.
selfHost.bullet2: Any number of servers, projects, CLIs
selfHost.bullet3: Your domain, your data, your backups

pro.name: Pro
pro.blurb: We host the master. You bring servers, keys, and CLIs.
pro.bullet2: Voice mode + full Claude Code & Gemini CLI integration

team.name: Team
team.blurb: Everything in Pro, plus shared infrastructure for small teams.
team.bullet2: Audit log of every agent action

pricing.lockInNote: No lock-in. Cancel anytime. Export your data anytime. Self-host any time.
pricing.footer: Either way, your Claude/Gemini key or subscription stays yours. We don't resell anyone's tokens.

security.q: Is it secure? Where do my API keys go?
security.a1: Your secrets stay in your infrastructure. Period. In self-host: keys live in your Postgres, never leave your network. We literally cannot see them. In cloud: keys are encrypted at rest with libsodium sealed boxes, decrypted only at the moment an agent starts a task. Even our database admin cannot read them.
security.a2: The agent on your server runs in a sandboxed namespace. It physically cannot access ~/.claude/* or read other processes' memory. Verify it yourself: every line is open source under AGPL-3.0.

noVpn.q: Does it work without a VPN?
noVpn.a1: Yes. Self-host on your own VPS — the UI talks directly to your server. The cloud version is also accessible without a VPN.
noVpn.a2: One caveat: Google geo-blocks Gemini CLI for some regions. If your server isn't in a blocked region, Gemini works fine. Nothing happens on the device side — the call goes server ↔ Anthropic / Google.

cliSupport.a1: Today: Claude Code and Gemini CLI in production.
cliSupport.a2: In progress: Codex CLI (OpenAI), Aider, Cursor CLI. Want to add your own? Open an issue or a PR — the architecture is extensible by design.

anthropic.q: How is this different from Anthropic Remote Control?
anthropic.a1: Anthropic is bound to Claude only and to an Anthropic subscription. Autmzr supports any CLI, any server, no vendor lock-in. Plus we're self-hostable — Anthropic Remote Control runs only through their infrastructure.
anthropic.a2: If Claude on a single device is enough for you, Anthropic Remote Control is fine. If you have multiple servers or want other CLIs, that's where we come in.

ports.q: Do I need to expose ports on my servers?
ports.a1: No. The agent initiates an outbound WebSocket connection to the master — nothing inbound, no exposed ports, no Cloudflare tunnel required.

asyncTask.q: What happens to my task if the agent disconnects?
asyncTask.a1: The task keeps running on your server. When the device comes back online, you see the buffered output and current state. Unlike Telegram bots that die at the 5-minute mark with a fake "✅ done".

teams.a1: Right now: single user, single infrastructure. Teams, SSO, audit log and RBAC are on the roadmap, not today.

footer.tagline: All your VPS and CLIs in one mobile interface.
footer.made: Made with ☕ in {year} by a solo developer · AGPL-3.0

showcase.title: Phone first.
showcase.titleAccent: Desktop is just a wider mobile.
showcase.lede: Most Claude UIs are desktop apps with phone-shaped CSS bolted on. We did it the other way: every screen drawn on a 390-pixel phone first, then allowed to breathe on a laptop.
showcase.desktopTitle: Open it on a laptop. Same app, just wider.

mocks.chatMsg2: Reading src/api/upload.ts. I'll wrap with try/catch and return a structured error.
mocks.chatMsg3: Done. Want a test for the failure path?
mocks.subscriptionTagline: Pro — $19/mo. Unlimited servers, unlimited projects, voice mode, full Claude Code & Gemini integration.

billing.tagline: Pro — $19/mo. Unlimited servers, unlimited projects, voice mode, full Claude Code & Gemini integration.
billing.comingSoonBody: We're integrating the payment provider. During beta all users stay on trial. We'll email {email} when billing goes live.

terminal.label: terminal
terminal.intro: Commands run on the project's device.

pty.debugTitle: Copy raw PTY output (debug URL parser)
pty.debugCopied: Copied raw buffer ({count} chars) to clipboard. Paste in chat if URL doesn't parse.
pty.ctrlTitle: Next key will be with Ctrl
pty.alertNotSentWs:
❌ Not sent: WebSocket {state}

Text is still in modal buffer. Close modal and reopen PTY (session may have dropped).
pty.pasteModalGotFromBuffer: ✓ got from buffer — check and tap "Paste"
pty.pasteModalLongPress: Long-press in field below → "Paste" in iOS menu

invites.title: Invite codes
invites.emptyHint: You don't have any invite codes yet. Create one and share the link with whoever you want to invite.

claudeLogin.title: Sign in to Claude
claudeLogin.alertBadApiKey:
API key must start with sk-ant-…
Copy it from console.anthropic.com/settings/keys
claudeLogin.alertNotJson: This doesn't look like JSON. Copy the whole file.
claudeLogin.subInstructionsTitle: How to sign in via subscription
claudeLogin.subStep1: claude /login will start below — a URL will appear.
claudeLogin.subStep2: Long-press the URL → "Copy link" → tap → Safari opens claude.ai.
claudeLogin.subStep3: Sign in, claude.ai gives you a code. Copy it.
claudeLogin.subStep4: Come back here → 📋 Paste in the top corner of the terminal → paste code → "Paste ↵".
claudeLogin.copyCredsHint: Copy a ready credentials.json from your computer:
claudeLogin.apiKeyHowTo: How to get an API key
claudeLogin.apiKeyStep2: Click Create Key, give it a name (like "autmzr-command")
claudeLogin.apiKeyStep4: Paste below and click "Save". The key uses pay-per-use, not subscription.
claudeLogin.oauthWarning: OAuth via claude auth login waits for callback on localhost of the same device — won't work on remote server. Use "Subscription" (copy credentials.json) or "API key".

claudeInstall.title: Install Claude Code
claudeInstall.timeout: timeout — install is taking longer than 10 minutes
claudeInstall.nextStep: Next — "Sign in to Claude" so the CLI can respond.
claudeInstall.failedHint: Check the log above — usually no node/npm or sudo needed.

geminiSetup.title: Gemini CLI setup
geminiSetup.installDescription: One command installs @google/gemini-cli on the device. Requires Node.js 20+.
geminiSetup.alreadyInstalled: Gemini is already installed. You can update or go to API key on the right.
geminiSetup.installedHint: Installed. Now add the API key on the right.
geminiSetup.keyDescriptionSuffix: . Free tier: 1500 requests per day, no card.
geminiSetup.keyHint: Key is written to systemd-override + ~/.bashrc on the device. NOT saved in our DB.
geminiSetup.keySavedHint: Done — Gemini is set up. You can now chat by picking "Gemini" in the agent selector.
